source_message: Oracle 06-24 NY-AM: approved MNQ B Inversion long as a valid first trade that stopped first. User specifically asked why the bot did not take the second trade; trace shows later long candidates confirmed after the 10:20 stop, including a 10:32 EDT long candidate before the 10:46/10:48 target run, but current engine policy kills later confirmed walkers with session_primary_already_taken after the first packet_ready. Preserve this as the key implementation lesson; do not relabel the first trade as a win.

oracle_context.approval_note: User approved 2026-07-02: first MNQ B trade is valid and stopped; second-entry behavior is the real lesson and should be documented for future implementation review.
oracle_context.open_reaction: AS.L rejection -> bullish divergent; B cap

paired_mes_context.fresh_fold: context none / no setup; brief reason no_bias; pillar2 marginal

second_entry_review.why_no_second_trade: The first 09:51 packet advanced its walker to packet_ready. Current deterministic strategy has a one-primary-packet NY-AM session latch; later confirmed walkers are killed with session_primary_already_taken instead of becoming executable packets.
second_entry_review.probable_valid_second_entry_note: A later Inversion long confirmed at 10:32 EDT after the 10:20 stop and before the 10:46 TP1 / 10:48 TP2 run. User notes this second entry would probably have been valid and won. It is not currently an oracle trade because it never reached packet_ready under the one-primary-session latch.

blocked_later_candidates.note: probable second-entry candidate before target run

second_entry_review.future_work: Design and test controlled retry-after-true-execution-stop behavior separately; do not solve it by rewriting this first trade outcome.
